intent.raw_request: Cut KStack 1.0.0 as a clean self-hosted KStack release.
goals: Make the KStack repository self-hosting from committed repo state and committed main-branch contracts.
Keep the public workflow surface limited to /kstack and its supported routed subcommands.
Align CI, docs, install scripts, and release metadata with the actual shipped V1 product.
Leave main ready for further KStack-driven development without reintroducing bootstrap or compatibility scaffolding.
non_goals: Adding new public routed commands beyond the supported /kstack surface.
Expanding V1 into npm publishing or non-Codex host support.
Taking on backlog items like richer readiness narration, contract diffs, or PR-sync helpers in this release.
constraints: All persistent repo-facing text must remain in English.
main must keep only .kstack/state/main.json as raw tracked branch state.
Feature branches may carry raw branch state temporarily, but that raw state must not survive merge to main.
Durable .kstack/contracts artifacts remain the committed branch-history surface after merge.
Source-checkout installation remains the canonical distribution model for V1.
candidate_wedges: Enforce a deterministic main contract and self-hosting invariant check.
Strip browser command tables from root /kstack help while keeping advanced runtime docs in BROWSER.md.
Replace stale GitHub eval matrices with validation workflows that match the real repo.

sprint.problem_statement: KStack needs a clean self-hosted V1 baseline so further KStack work can happen entirely through KStack workflow and committed repo artifacts.
in_scope_behavior: Commit and validate main branch state plus committed main branch contract artifacts.
Keep /kstack help limited to the supported routed workflow surface.
Align CI and release metadata with the actual V1 repo surface.
Make setup and uninstall safe for a self-hosting repository that tracks its own state.
out_of_scope_behavior: Adding new routed workflow commands or reviving removed legacy surfaces.
Implementing post-V1 backlog items such as contract diffs, PR-body sync helpers, or browser ergonomics enhancements.
Expanding KStack beyond its Codex-first source-checkout distribution model.
acceptance_checks: .kstack/state/main.json exists, validates, and reflects the V1 self-hosting baseline.
.kstack/contracts/main.json and .md exist, are deterministic, and stay fresh against main state.
Root /kstack help exposes only the supported routed workflow and not browser command tables.
GitHub validation references only shipped V1 surfaces and current tests.
Setup and uninstall remain safe for a repository that tracks its own workflow state.
VERSION and package metadata are aligned on 1.0.0.
escalation_triggers: Main contract freshness cannot be checked deterministically from committed state.
The repo needs to keep raw feature-branch state on main after merge.
The public /kstack surface cannot be cleaned without breaking supported QA workflow.

routing.reason: The self-hosted V1 baseline spans workflow core, docs, CI, and release surfaces.

readiness.summary: The branch contract is current and no semantic blockers remain.
